---
title: "Jiregna Olani Kedida"
---

::: {.hero-section}
# Jiregna Olani Kedida

## Biostatistics | Clinical Epidemiology | Public Health | Artificial Intelligence

I am a PhD candidate in **Biostatistics, Epidemiology, and Public Health** at the University of Padova and a Lecturer in Statistics at **Ambo University, Ethiopia**. My work focuses on statistical modeling, epidemiological methods, machine learning, federated learning, and data-driven public health research.

[About Me](about.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary} [Publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::

## Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health AI Lab

The long-term vision is to build a research and training platform that supports students, researchers, clinicians, and public health professionals in Ethiopia and beyond.

## Research Focus

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-4 .card-box}
### Biostatistics
Statistical modeling, regression methods, Bayesian analysis, and clinical prediction models.
:::

::: {.g-col-4 .card-box}
### Clinical Epidemiology
Maternal and child health, disease burden, survival analysis, and public health evidence.
:::

::: {.g-col-4 .card-box}
### Public Health AI
Machine learning, federated learning, reproducible research, and AI for health systems.
:::
:::

## Current Academic Direction

My current research interests include Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling, malaria burden estimation, maternal and newborn health analytics, interpretable machine learning, and privacy-preserving federated learning.
